summary: Small conflict-resolution PR for Mario fighter code. It updated Mario-specific special-attribute field accesses in src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.c from direct Fighter fields x222C/x2230 to the fighter special-attributes union path sa.mario.x222C/sa.mario.x2230, preserving existing random selection logic while aligning with the newer Fighter struct layout.
what_changed: In _func_800E0EE0_arr_copy, comparisons against ft_2->x222C and ft_2->x2230 were changed to ft_2->sa.mario.x222C and ft_2->sa.mario.x2230.
In func_800E0D1C, the loop filtering previous Mario indices now reads ft->sa.mario.x222C/x2230 instead of direct Fighter fields.
In func_800E0D1C, updates that shift x222C into x2230 and store a new random index now write through ft->sa.mario.x2230 and ft->sa.mario.x222C.
In func_800E0EE0, the same Mario state update on ft_2 was changed from direct fields to ft_2->sa.mario.x2230 and ft_2->sa.mario.x222C.
No algorithmic behavior is evident from the diff; the change is a layout/access-path fix after a merge conflict involving Mario progress and the fighter special attributes union.
smart_moves: Resolved the conflict by preserving the existing Mario random-index exclusion/update logic and only changing the field access path to the special-attributes union.

Applied the sa.mario qualifier consistently across helper and caller code, avoiding a partial migration where reads and writes could diverge between old direct fields and new character-specific storage.
Kept the narrow fix to one file and one field-family, reducing risk in a fast conflict-resolution merge.
decomp_lessons: When Fighter character-specific state moves into the special-attributes union, update all reads and writes together; direct fields like ft->x222C/ft->x2230 may become ft->sa.<character>.x222C/x2230.
Conflict fixes in decomp code often need to reconcile progress in matched C with parallel struct/header refactors rather than changing generated behavior.

For character files such as ftmario.c, ambiguous raw offsets should be accessed through the character-specific union member once that member exists, even if the leaf field names remain unknown offsets.
naming_conventions: Mario-specific fighter special attributes are accessed as ft->sa.mario.<field>.
Unidentified Mario state fields retained offset-style names x222C and x2230 under the mario special-attributes namespace.
Helper name _func_800E0EE0_arr_copy and functions func_800E0D1C/func_800E0EE0 remained unchanged; this PR did not introduce semantic names for the affected fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The diff suggests a non-behavioral struct access-path change intended to keep existing matched logic compatible with a new Fighter layout.
Array filtering over indices 0..8, HSD_Randi(outpos), and assignment ordering were preserved exactly, which is useful for maintaining matching while updating struct definitions.
No explicit assembly, register, or match-percentage discussion was present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Look for the PR or commit that introduced Fighter::sa.mario and moved x222C/x2230 into the Mario special-attributes union.
Search later ftmario.c history for semantic names replacing sa.mario.x222C and sa.mario.x2230.
Check whether other character files needed similar conflict fixes from direct Fighter fields to sa.<character> union fields.
